OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 57974220) FILED BY PLAINTIFF JB5, INC.,A CORPORATION DBA ROYAL HAWAIIAN SEAFOOD

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS MMP RESTAURANTS, LLC, GIUSEPPE SPINOSO, GIUSEPPE SCOPPETTA, AND MICHELE DI RUOCCO'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANTS MMP RESTAURANTS, LLC AND MICHELE DI RUOCCO. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO MMP RESTAURANTS, LLC BECAUSE COUNSEL'S FAILURE TO CHANGE THE AGENT FOR SERVICE OF PROCESS MAY HAVE CONTRIBUTED TO THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AGAINST THAT ENTITY. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANTS RUOCCO, CAFE TIRAMISU, BRINDISI CUCINA DE MARE, AND BELDON TAVERNA PER CCP 473.5. THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS, WHO WERE ACTING FOR THOSE RESTAURANTS, DECLARE THAT THEY DID NOT RECEIVE LEGAL PROCESS AND LACKED ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE ACTION. ALL DEFENDANTS AND COUNSEL PALENCHAR ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE TO PLAINTIFF FOR $1,500 AS THE REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS FOR OBTAINING THE DEFAULTS. SEE ROGALSKI V. NABERS CADILLAC (1992) 11 CAL.APP.4TH 816, 822-823. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO DEFENDANTS GIUSEPPE SPINOSO AND GIUSEPPE SCOPPETTA. THE COURT FINDS THOSE TWO DEFENDANTS WERE SERVED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 15337049): HIGGINBOTHAM, EDWARD M. SUBSTITUTED FOR PALENCHAR, RICHARD JOHN AS ATTORNEY FOR MMP RESTAURANTS, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY DBA CAFE TIRAMISU AND ALSO DBA BRINDISI CUCINA DE MARE AND ALSO DBA BELDEN TAVERNA

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DENIS KLAVDIANOS, ESQ. of Glassberg, Pollak & Associates, Tel No. 415-291-8320 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. RICHARD J. PALENCHAR, ESQ. of Palenchar Law Firm, Tel No. 415-515-2368 appearing for the moving party defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling, and ruled as follows: DEFENDANTS MMP RESTAURANTS, LLC, GIUSEPPE SPINOSO, GIUSEPPE SCOPPETTA, AND MICHELE DI RUOCCO'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to defendants MMP Restaurants, LLC and Michele Di Ruocco. The motion is granted as to MMP Restaurants, LLC because counsel's failure to change the agent for service of process may have contributed to the entry of default against that entity. The motion is granted as to defendants Ruocco, Cafe Tiramisu, Brindisi Cucina De Mare, and Beldon Taverna per CCP 473.5. The individual defendants, who were acting for those restaurants, declare that they did not receive legal process and lacked actual notice of the action. All Defendants and counsel Palenchar are jointly and severally liable to plaintiff for $1,500 as the reasonable fees and costs for obtaining the defaults. See Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822-823. The motion is denied as to defendants Giuseppe Spinoso and Giuseppe Scoppetta. The Court finds those two defendants were served. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
